QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_65

question.text: What is the lock-in period for excess promoter holding (beyond minimum contribution) when the majority of issue proceeds are used for capital expenditure?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When the majority of issue proceeds (excluding OFS) are used for capital expenditure, the excess promoter holding (beyond minimum contribution) is locked in for 1 year. This is longer than the standard 6-month lock-in for excess holding. **The Trap:** 6 months is the standard lock-in for excess promoter holding in normal issues. 3 years is the lock-in for minimum contribution (not excess) in capex-heavy issues. Candidates must distinguish between minimum and excess contribution lock-ins. **Cross-Exam Context:** The distinction between minimum and excess promoter holding lock-ins is a nuanced area tested in NISM Series IX. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In capex-heavy IPOs, both lock-in periods are roughly doubled: minimum goes from 18 months to 3 years, and excess goes from 6 months to 1 year.
